headline: Where the ranks moved.
standfirst: Of 31 keywords being tracked for the UAE database, four sit in the top 100 today — same count as engagement start, but the composition has shifted and the next round of signals (hreflang + schema, deployed June 1) hasn't been re-measured yet. The shape underneath is moving even when the headline rank count holds.

feature_chart.title: Position distribution across the tracked keyword set
feature_chart.subtitle: Count of tracked keywords per ranking band, monthly
feature_chart.caption: The flat horizontal line is the bottom band — 27 keywords still outside the top 100 — and it tells the most honest story. The work we deployed June 1 (hreflang + Organization schema + 586 disavow) has not had a re-measurement window yet. Reindex typically takes 14 to 28 days; expect the bottom band to start dropping on the July 1 reading.

prose.title: Why the bands look frozen.
paragraphs: Position tracking is a lagging indicator by design. The visibility score on the Domain Overview page moved within a week of deployment because Google read the new structured data into the index quickly. But ranking changes for individual keywords require Google to re-evaluate the page against a query — which only happens at the next crawl-and-rank cycle.

For a domain with Authority Score 25 and the commercial-intent vertical we operate in, that cycle averages 14 to 28 days post-deploy. We deployed June 1. The next meaningful re-measurement should be the July 1 reading.
The 27-keyword off-index list is the priority watch. If even three of them break into the top 100 in July, the trajectory works. If none do, we revisit content depth on those specific pages.

pull_quote.text: If three off-index keywords break into the top 100 in July, the trajectory works.
